issues: palette_saturation_mismatch: peers use desaturated metallic grays/silvers; candidate is dominated by vivid blues and pure white — chain armor expected to be gray/silver metallic

representation_type_divergence: peers are flat equipment-icon sprites (helmets, shields); candidate is a full standing humanoid character figure — even ignoring item-type differences, this is a structural rendering inconsistency
color_temperature_drift: candidate introduces warm blonde/yellow hair tones not present anywhere in peer palette
highlight_style: peers use tight specular highlights on convex metal surfaces; candidate highlights are broad flat fabric-style whites inconsistent with chain mail rendering
charm_level: peers are inert item icons with zero personality; candidate is an expressive humanoid figure — charm gap is category-wide and significant
outline_color: peers use near-black (#1a1a1a range) outlines consistently; candidate outline appears slightly softer/warmer, subtle but detectable divergence
detail_density: candidate's character anatomy (hair, face, boots) introduces detail dimensions entirely absent from peer icon set — density qualitatively higher
reasoning: Peer set is classic RPG item-icon sprites: armor pieces and shields rendered as isolated objects with muted metallic palettes, tight 1px near-black outlines, and minimal personality. Candidate armor_chain_v00 presents as a full standing humanoid character dressed in vivid blue-and-white robes/armor with blonde hair — a fundamentally different representational form. Ignoring item-type per instructions, the style breaks are: (1) palette is wrong for chain — vivid saturated blue vs expected metallic gray; (2) highlight language is cloth/fabric rather than interlinked metal rings; (3) warm hair tones pollute an otherwise cool-metallic peer palette; (4) charm delta is high — a standing character with face and flowing cape reads several notches above the neutral item-icon charm level of peers. Outline weight and shadow direction are the only clean passes. Six of eleven dimensions show meaningful discontinuity.
